reforms.description: Latvia aims to more than double its R&D spending from 0.6% to 1.5% of GDP by 2020, addressing the low levels of public and private expenditure on research and development.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increasing R&D spending is expected to enhance innovation capacity and productivity, contributing positively to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The authorities have an ambitious goal of more than doubling the R&D spending.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant structural change in the funding landscape for R&D, aiming to significantly boost innovation and competitiveness.
reforms.source_quote: The authorities have an ambitious goal of more than doubling the spending, from current 0.6% of GDP to 1.5% of GDP by 2020.

reforms.description: A new tax incentive has been introduced to stimulate investment in research, development, and innovation, allowing firms to decrease their corporate income tax liability based on the costs of purchased intangibles.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The tax incentive is designed to encourage firms to invest in R&D, which can lead to increased innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: A new tax incentive has been recently introduced to stimulate investment in research, development and innovation.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform introduces a significant new mechanism for encouraging private sector investment in R&D, although its effectiveness will depend on implementation.
reforms.source_quote: A new tax incentive has been recently introduced to stimulate investment in research, development and innovation.

reforms.description: A consolidation of the existing public research infrastructure is underway, aiming to improve the alignment of research activities with business needs and enhance international cooperation.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving research infrastructure is expected to enhance the productivity and innovation capacity of local enterprises.
reforms.status_evidence: A consolidation of the existing public research infrastructure is underway.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant structural change in the public research landscape, aiming to better align research with market needs and enhance innovation.
reforms.source_quote: A consolidation of the existing public research infrastructure is underway.

reforms.description: Latvia aims to develop incentives for international cooperation in local research and innovation, alongside a regular external evaluation exercise.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Encouraging international collaboration can enhance knowledge transfer and innovation, leading to improved research outcomes and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: Develop incentives for international cooperation in local research and innovation.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative is significant as it aims to enhance Latvia's research landscape through international collaboration, which is vital for innovation.
reforms.source_quote: Develop incentives for international cooperation in local research and innovation as well as a regular external evaluation exercise.

reforms.description: Latvia aims to design and implement an effective research and innovation policy that upgrades infrastructure and rationalizes research institutions while encouraging companies to innovate, addressing the low levels of public funding for R&D.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increasing public funding for R&D is expected to enhance scientific progress and innovation capacity, which can lead to higher productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The policy aim is to design and implement an effective research and innovation policy.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in Latvia's approach to R&D funding, addressing a critical gap in public investment that has hindered scientific progress and innovation.
reforms.source_quote: The amount of public funding provided for R&D is the lowest of any EU member state and the lack of public funding is identified by the Ministry of Education as a major factor slowing down scientific progress in the country.

reforms.description: Latvia's Smart Specialisation Strategy aims to enhance knowledge transfer and increase funding for R&D to 1.5% of GDP, with a focus on priority areas such as biomedicine and ICT.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By directing funding towards high-potential sectors, the strategy is expected to stimulate innovation and productivity growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The strategy has been produced as part of applying for EU regional policy.
reforms.importance_rationale: This strategy represents a significant shift in Latvia's approach to R&D funding and sectoral focus, aiming to enhance competitiveness and innovation capacity.
reforms.source_quote: The Smart Specialisation Strategy focuses on effective coordination between knowledge specialisation and capabilities of industries, increasing the knowledge pool, policies promoting absorption of knowledge and knowledge transfer.

reforms.description: The National Industrial Policy aims to diversify Latvia's economic growth sources by boosting the manufacturing sector's role and productivity.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By enhancing the manufacturing sector, the policy is expected to create jobs and improve overall economic productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The National Industrial Policy has been adopted as a reflection on the pre-crisis sources of growth.
reforms.importance_rationale: This policy represents a fundamental shift in Latvia's economic strategy, focusing on manufacturing as a key driver of growth and productivity.
reforms.source_quote: The stated policy goal is to increase the share of manufacturing in the economy from 14% in 2011 to 20% in 2020.

reforms.description: The Latvian government has reformed its tax incentives for R&D, making them more flexible and generous to encourage private sector investment in research.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By enhancing tax incentives, the reform aims to stimulate private sector R&D investment, which can lead to increased innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The authorities have recently made the second tax incentive significantly more flexible and generous.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant as it modifies existing tax incentives to better support R&D activities, potentially increasing private sector engagement in innovation.
reforms.source_quote: In response, the authorities have recently made the second tax incentive significantly more flexible and generous.

reforms.description: Latvia has established six Competence Centres in various sectors to provide direct support for R&D and innovation, particularly targeting young start-ups.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By fostering collaboration between public and private sectors, these centres aim to enhance innovation capacity and support the growth of start-ups.
reforms.status_evidence: A plethora of direct R&D support programmes is available, subsidized largely by EU structural funds.
reforms.importance_rationale: The establishment of Competence Centres represents a significant effort to enhance R&D support for specific sectors, which can lead to increased innovation and economic activity.
reforms.source_quote: Six so-called Competence Centres have been established in the following sectors: pharmaceuticals and chemicals, wood, environment, bioenergy and biotechnologies, electrical and optical equipment, ICT, transport, and engineering.

reforms.description: Latvia established a Research and Innovation Strategic Council to oversee a structural reform aimed at consolidating existing research infrastructure and integrating research into the tertiary education sector.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The reform aims to enhance the efficiency and effectiveness of research infrastructure, which can lead to improved innovation outcomes and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The authorities are launching a structural reform to improve research and innovation.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in the governance and structure of research in Latvia, aiming to enhance the overall research capacity and its integration with education.
reforms.source_quote: The authorities recently established a Research and Innovation Strategic Council under the direct supervision of the Prime Minister and are launching a structural reform.

reforms.description: A comprehensive reform of vocational education and training (VET) is underway in Latvia, involving the streamlining of VET institutions and the introduction of a voucher programme to encourage lifelong learning.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By improving vocational education and promoting lifelong learning, the reform aims to enhance the skills of the workforce, which is essential for innovation and productivity growth.
reforms.status_evidence: A comprehensive reform of vocational education and training is underway.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform addresses critical issues in the education system and aims to align vocational training with labor market needs, which is vital for economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: A comprehensive reform of vocational education and training is underway.

reforms.description: Latvia aims to support the development of knowledge markets by providing firms with well-defined and high-quality intellectual property rights, which is essential for fostering innovation and ensuring that research outputs can be effectively commercialized.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing intellectual property rights can incentivize firms to invest in innovation, leading to increased productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The strategy includes plans to support knowledge markets through improved intellectual property rights.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant as it addresses a critical aspect of the innovation ecosystem, which is the protection and commercialization of intellectual property, but it does not represent a complete overhaul of the system.
reforms.source_quote: Support the development of knowledge markets by providing firms with well-defined and high quality intellectual property rights.

reforms.description: Latvia plans to consolidate its existing research infrastructure and develop incentives for international cooperation in local research and innovation, which is expected to enhance the overall research capacity and collaboration.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving research infrastructure and fostering international collaboration can lead to enhanced research outputs and innovation, contributing positively to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The initiative includes plans for consolidating research infrastructure and promoting international cooperation.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant as it aims to strengthen the research infrastructure, which is crucial for innovation, but it does not represent a fundamental change in the system.
reforms.source_quote: Consolidate the existing research infrastructure. Develop incentives to international cooperation in local research and innovation.

reforms.description: Latvia intends to evaluate the newly reformed tax incentives for R&D, particularly focusing on their effectiveness in reaching young firms, which is crucial for fostering innovation in the startup ecosystem.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Evaluating and potentially improving tax incentives can enhance R&D investment, particularly among young firms, thereby supporting innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The evaluation of tax incentives for R&D is part of the broader strategy to enhance innovation.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant as it addresses the effectiveness of tax incentives in promoting R&D, which is vital for innovation, but it does not constitute a major overhaul.
reforms.source_quote: Evaluate the newly reformed tax incentives for R&D, also in view to reaching young firms.
